mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals tightening labor market and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. This mechanism produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.6% as of May 2013 reflects ongoing labor market slack
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE momentum and 20-day forward returns exceeds 0.05 in absolute value, or negative returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines by >0.2pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal accelerating residential investment and consumer confidence, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth. This mechanism produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the earnings cycle strengthens.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 853k units in April 2013 reflects housing recovery momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST 3-month growth and 60-day forward returns is below 0.04 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 58% of instances when HOUST rises >5% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) begins to accelerate, creating negative real rates that incentivize equity risk-taking and asset price appreciation. This mechanism produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking real returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.11% and CPIAUCSL at 231.485 as of May 2013
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with emerging inflation
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 60% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates below its 3-month trend, signaling weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints that reduce corporate output capacity. This mechanism produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate earnings disappointments.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances when INDPRO declines, or Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) accelerates above its 6-month trend while payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously rises, signaling synchronized demand and labor market strength. This joint signal produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the earnings cycle enters an expansion phase.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 421.147B and PAYEMS at 135.637M in May 2013
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 62% of instances, or Rank IC of the joint signal is below 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates above 0.2% month-over-month, signaling robust labor demand that raises consumer purchasing power and corporate revenue expectations. This mechanism produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135.637M in May 2013 reflects ongoing job creation
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 56% of instances when PAYEMS grows >0.2% month-over-month, or Rank IC is below 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (approximated as FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation) turn negative and remain below -1.5%, signaling financial repression that forces investors into equities to preserve real wealth. This mechanism produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as asset allocation shifts toward equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Negative real rates persist in 2013 post-crisis environment
regime_hint: zero lower bound with financial repression
failure_condition: When real rates are below -1.5%, positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 64% of instances, or Rank IC is below 0.07 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle slowdown and rising recession risk. This mechanism produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice equity risk premiums upward in anticipation of earnings contraction.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of instances when GDPC1 growth falls below 0.5% annualized, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
